N0.28414/2023 (Stay) For hearing of main case 18.12.?0231 Mr. Rafiq Ahmed Kalwar, advocate for lhe petitioner. 1. Granted 3. Granted subject to alljust exceptions. 2, 4-5. While confronted as to maintainability of instant petition as the petitioner js civil servant and has a remedy to file representalion before the competent authority, learned counsel for the petitioner has attempted to argue lhat since the entire proceedings against the petitioner are based on malafide, whereas, a very minor offence of wrong parking of the vehicle the petitioner has been given major penally without adopting lhe legal course, therefore, petitioner has approached this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution of lslamic Republic of Pakistan. 1973, as the Principle of Natural Justice has been violated and the petitioner has been awarded maximum punishment of dismissal from service while referring to the FlRs, which were not even the part of the Show Cause Notice or Charge Sheet for whjch the petitioner has already been acquitted. According to learned counsel for the petitioner. since ihis is a case of depriving the fair trial and violation of Principle of Natural Justice, therefore, bar under Article 212 of the constitution of lslamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 was not attracted. According to learned counsel for the petitioner. since ihis is a case of depriving the fair trial and violation of Principle of Natural Justice, therefore, bar under Article 212 of the constitution of lslamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 was not attracted. Prima-facie, from perusal of the record placed beiore lhis Court and the Show Cause Notice issued to the petitioner, it appears that petitioner has been removed from the service on lhe charges that he has parked his 1 3. 4. 5 @ l vehicle in wrong side at No Parking Zone, whereafter, some harsh words were exchanged and there seems no allegation with regard to have chequered history of the petitioner relating to FlRs, wherein, according to learned counsel for the petitioner, he has been acquitted and inspile of such fact, awalding oI major penalty to the petitioner under the circumstances, removal from service seems to be reasonable. however. we are still of the opinion that since the petitioner is not remediless as the forums are available to seek redressal of the grievance in accordance with law, therefore, it will be premature lo examine these facts and be treated valid ground for filing instant Constitutional Petitlon without availing the remedy available by filing representation/appeal before lhe relevant forum in accordance with law. While confronted with hereinabove position, learned counsel for petitioner does not press instant petition and will seek remedy available to the petitioner before the relevant forum in accordance with law. Accordingly, petition stands disposed of along with listed application ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE JUDGE \2!9eE
